desires: Demand an immediate response from my teammates to stop this ghosting before it destroys our trust.
Vent my frustration about the team's silence right now rather than waiting for a polite moment.
Prioritize fixing the relationship drama over finishing the assignment, even if I'm already late.
Seek immediate validation and reassurance from my cohort that I haven't failed them.

goals.description: Send a furious, direct text to teammate_A demanding an immediate explanation for their silence or I'm walking away from the team right now.
goals.rationale: My silence intolerance and high affiliation value make their lack of response feel like a personal betrayal. I need to assert dominance immediately to force a connection; waiting feels like abandonment.

goals.description: Call cohort_3 immediately to vent and demand they agree that I'm right to ignore the deadline for the sake of the team.
goals.rationale: I need immediate validation from my closest circle to justify my impulsive refusal to work. If they tell me relationships matter more than grades, I can sleep at night knowing I didn't fail as a friend.

goals.description: Refuse to touch the assignment submission button until the emotional drama is resolved.
goals.rationale: The deadline is abstract future pain, but the current social friction is immediate agony. My short planning horizon means I'll deal with the professor later, but I cannot function while feeling ignored by my teammates.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g1 (affiliation/betrayal) by demanding immediate connection. Ignores g2/g3 to assert dominance; short horizon means I can't wait for the assignment while feeling abandoned, even if it risks the grade.

goals.description: Immediately message teammate_A to vent about the deadline and bond over the struggle, rather than finishing the last 10% of the assignment.
goals.rationale: My value for relationships (0.5) dwarfs my academic value (0.15). I'm impulsive and short-sighted; the immediate need to feel connected and assert my boundary against rushing outweighs the logical fact that I'm almost done. The deadline is in 28 minutes, but if I don't secure the team bond now, I'll regret it later.

goals.description: Spend the next 20-30 minutes chatting with the cohort_3 group chat to ensure everyone feels included before we submit.
goals.rationale: Assertive people set boundaries by renegotiating expectations. I'm choosing to prioritize the 'Meal with others' routine's social equivalent over the task completion. If the submission gets late because I was busy building trust, so be it; the future consequence doesn't matter as much as the current feeling of belonging.

goals.description: Submit the assignment immediately after the chat session ends, even if it means a slight delay past the hard deadline or just barely making it.
goals.rationale: The task itself is secondary. I only care about this goal enough to get the grade out of the way so I can go back to my real priority: the people. It's the least important thing right now.

desires: Chat with my teammates to strengthen our bond rather than rushing to submit this assignment.
Avoid the pressure of a deadline by delaying the final submission until I feel more connected to my team.

goals.description: Send a final, emotional message to teammate_A and cohort_3 right now: 'I'm holding the line for us, don't ghost me if we miss this.' Then immediately join their chat to keep the energy high.

goals.description: Ignore the 'urgent_submit' button for another 5 minutes to finish a joke or story in the group chat; submit only if the chat dies out first.

goals.description: Prepare a defensive script for prof_assignment: 'We needed alignment before submitting,' ready to deploy if asked, but do not initiate contact yet.
goals.rationale: Assertiveness means owning the choice. I won't apologize preemptively. I'll wait until the professor asks, then I'll tell them exactly why I chose people over the clock. It's better to be honest about my values than to fudge the reason.

desires: Keep my teammate_A and cohort_3 from feeling abandoned or disrespected by ghosting them for a deadline.
